title: A lens system
abstract: An imaging system (1) is provided comprising a variable focus lens (3), a variable angle beam deflector (4) and a variable iris (7), the system (1) further comprising an image sensor (8) arranged to capture images though the lens (3). The sensor (8) may comprise a 2D or ID array of sensing pixels. A control system may be arranged to receive data input and to output control signals controlling at least one of a deflection angle of the variable angle beam deflector (4), the optical power of the variable focus lens (3) and opening of the iris (7) and optionally an output of a lighting source (46). The imaging system (1) may be mounted on a part of a moving object such as a robot, drone or vehicle and may output signals to assist in guiding the moving object. The imaging system may provide one or more views for a driver, driver assistance system or for a partial or fully automated vehicle guidance system.
claims: Claims 1 . An imaging system comprising a variable focus lens and a variable angle beam deflector wherein the system further comprises a variable iris, the system further comprising an image sensor arranged to capture images though the lens. 2. An imaging system according to claim 1 wherein the variable lens comprise one of: (i) a liquid lens; (ii) a motorised lens; (iii) a telecentric lens. 3. An imaging system according to claim 1 or claim 2 wherein the variable angle beam deflector is one of: (i) a mirror having a drive arranged to change an angle of deflection; (ii) a prism adapted to change the angle of deflection. 4. An imaging system according to any preceding claim wherein the system further comprises a lighting source illuminating a field of view with visible or non- visible light. 5. An imaging system according to claim 4 wherein the lighting source is adapted to generate a spread of wavelengths of light or multiple wavelengths of light. 6. An imaging system according to claim 1 wherein the image sensor comprises a 2D array or I D array of sensing pixels or wherein the system is arranged such that a human eye is the image sensor. 7. An imaging system according to any preceding claim further comprising a control system arranged to receive data input and to output control signals and wherein the control system is arranged to output signals controlling at least one of a deflection angle of the variable beam deflector; an optical power of the variable focus lens; an opening of the iris. 8. An imaging system according to claim 7 as dependent on claim 4 wherein the control system is further arranged to output signals controlling an output of the lighting source. 9. An imaging system according to claim 7 or claim 8 wherein the system further comprises an object sensor arranged to detect an object and wherein the control system is arranged to set a direction of view of the variable focus lens and/or focus to image the object and wherein optionally the object sensor or a distance sensor is arranged to measure a distance to the object. 10. An imaging system according to any preceding claim wherein the system further comprises an image processing system. 1 1 . An imaging system in accordance with any preceding claim wherein the arrangement is adapted to image macroscopic objects or scenes by taking more than one image and then using those images as a larger composite image. 12. An imaging system in accordance with any one of claim 1 to 10 wherein the arrangement is arranged to image microscopic objects or scenes. 13. An imaging system in accordance with claim 1 1 or claim 12 wherein the sensor is a I D sensor and wherein the variable beam deflector is used to take multiple images in order to build up a 2D image. 14. An imaging system in accordance with any one of the preceding claims wherein the system is arranged to be used for at least one of: acquiring an image or view a scene of a moving object; tracking an object to reduce motion blur; outputting signals to assist in guiding a moving object; outputting control signals to adjusting for motion while the system is mounted on or is part of a moving object such as a robot or drone or vehicle. 1 5. An imaging system in accordance with any preceding claim wherein one or more imaging sensors are used to acquire images of a moving object from multiple directions. 16. An imaging system in accordance with any preceding claim wherein one or more imaging sensors are mounted on a vehicle for the purpose of at least one of: providing one or more views for the driver; providing one or more views for a driver assistance system; providing one or more views for a partial or fully automated vehicle guidance system. 17. A vehicle comprising an imaging system in accordance with any preceding claim. 1 8. A robot comprising an imaging system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 1 5 wherein the robot is arranged to use output data from the imaging system. 19. A robot according to claim 1 8 wherein output data from the imaging system is used to provide guidance information. 20. A robot according to claim 1 8 or 19 wherein the robot is a drone.
